name: hplan
description: 43 skills and 18 commands across 6 plugins for PMs who decide what, why, and how to build AI agents — from evidence gate (hplan) through PM lifecycle (discover → architect → deliver → measure → learn).

plugins.name: hplan
plugins.description: Evidence + COGS + Decision gate that runs BEFORE you commit to building. Mandatory interview evidence, executable COGS sentinel (p50/p90 margin with abuse blend), append-only Do-Not-Build exclusions registry, self-eval decision log, and multi-target handoff to Spec-Kit / Kiro / GStack / Claude Code.

plugins.name: discover
plugins.description: Discover which AI agent to build — map automation outcomes to solvable opportunities, validate assumptions, and estimate costs.

plugins.name: architect
plugins.description: Architect scalable AI agent systems — orchestration patterns, multi-agent design, memory architecture, business models, and competitive moats.

plugins.name: deliver
plugins.description: Deliver AI agents — instruction design, agent PRD templates, prompt engineering, context budgets, and sprint planning.

plugins.name: measure
plugins.description: Measure and optimize AI agent performance — KPIs, reliability, failure analysis, cost tracking, and North Star metrics.

plugins.name: learn
plugins.description: Turn PM tacit knowledge into reusable agent assets — structured knowledge units, decision patterns, and memory management.
